description: The Hongmen, formerly Tiandihui, is a Chinese fraternal organization and historically a secretive folk religious sect. Founded in 1761 in Fujian by Ti Xi and others, it originated as a Ming loyalist and anti-Qing society. After the Qing dynasty fell, some branches turned to crime. Today it is a secular fraternal organization, with some associations involved in united front activities.

country_links.evidence_quote: During the 19th century, branches of the Hongmen were formed by members of the Chinese diaspora in the United States, Canada and Australia...
country_links.context: Branches of the Hongmen were formed by members of the Chinese diaspora in Australia.

country_links.evidence_quote: During the 19th century, branches of the Hongmen were formed by members of the Chinese diaspora in the United States, Canada and Australia...
country_links.context: Branches of the Hongmen were formed by members of the Chinese diaspora in Canada, and a Canadian branch was established in 1863.

country_links.evidence_quote: Today, the Hongmen is an illegal society in Hong Kong because of its traditional association with the triads.
country_links.context: The Hongmen is an illegal society in Hong Kong because of its traditional association with the triads.

country_links.evidence_quote: In modern times, Hongmen associations, particularly in Taiwan, have been associated with united front activities.
country_links.context: Hongmen associations in Taiwan have been associated with united front activities and the organization is legal and politically influential there.

country_links.evidence_quote: During the 19th century, branches of the Hongmen were formed by members of the Chinese diaspora in the United States, Canada and Australia...
country_links.context: Branches of the Hongmen were formed by members of the Chinese diaspora in the United States.
